snippet.description:
When Ted Decker stepped in as CEO of The Home Depot in 2022, it was the best of times and the worst of times.  COVID-19 created a surge in home improvement projects alongside record sales. But this can be a tricky handoff for a CEO trying to make his mark.

In the latest episode of Exceptional Leaders, Retail analyst Simeon Gutman sits down with Decker to discuss the role of AI in the customer experience, how VR will scale productivity, and the energy the defines the company’s culture. https://mgstn.ly/3ZFZE5f

snippet.title: Dean's Speaker Series: Ted Decker, Chair, President, and CEO of The Home Depot
snippet.description:
Dean Maryam Alavi and Ted Decker, CEO of The Home Depot, sit down for a conversation about leadership and strategy, how The Home Depot plans to grow its business, and the future of retail and shopping. 

Ted Decker is chair, president, and CEO of The Home Depot, the world’s largest home improvement retailer with more than 2,300 stores and 500,000 associates. Under Ted’s leadership, The Home Depot is creating a seamless interconnected shopping experience for Pro and DIY customers, while championing the company’s unique culture.

snippet.title: The Home Depot
snippet.description:
The Home Depot, Inc., often simply referred to as Home Depot, is an American multinational home improvement retail corporation that sells tools, construction products, appliances, and services, including fuel and transportation rentals. Home Depot is the largest home improvement retailer in the United States.[3] In 2021, the company had 490,600 employees and more than $151 billion in revenue. The company is headquartered in incorporated Cobb County, Georgia, with an Atlanta mailing address.[4]

snippet.title: Flashback Forensics: Psychologist Analyzes Ted Bundy's Deception, Manipulation and Body Language
snippet.description:
In this episode of "Flashback Forensics", Dr. G delves deep into the mind of one of the most infamous serial killers, Ted Bundy. Leveraging her extensive expertise in clinical and forensic psychology, Dr. G meticulously analyzes two critical interviews of Bundy - one from a Colorado jail in 1977 and the other from Dr. James Dobson in 1989, just prior to Bundy's execution.

Join the waitlist now: https://www.drgexplains.com/waitlist-body-language-course

The 1977 interview reveals Bundy's attempts to manipulate perceptions, seemingly trying to convince the world of his innocence, while the 1989 interview shows his desperate bid to sway Dr. Dobson, perhaps hoping to obtain a stay of execution.

Throughout this in-depth analysis, Dr. G dissects not only the words spoken by Bundy, but also his body language, shedding light on the often subtle cues that betray his true intentions and thoughts. Marcus, a co-founder of Home Depot, publicly endorsed Trump, who is currently the frontrunner for the 2024 Republican nomination.

Trump is confronting four criminal cases, encompassing both state and federal charges related to his attempts to overturn the 2020 election results. The legal uncertainties surrounding his campaign have raised questions about the impact of a potential conviction or imprisonment on his political future.

According to Marcus, Trump expressed happiness regarding the continued support. This insight into their conversation provides a glimpse into the personal dynamics between the mega-donor and the political figure.
